Button.description: Triggers an action or navigation. Always labelled. The primary driver of user flow in Meesho.
doRules: Use Primary (Jamun/purple) for the single most important action on the screen.
Use Secondary-Grey for confirmatory/cancel actions alongside a primary.
Use Tertiary-Link for low-emphasis actions like 'See all' or 'Skip'.
Icon buttons must have a visible selected state (line icon → filled icon).
Minimum touch target is 48dp height even for small buttons.
dontRules: Don't stack two Primary buttons on the same screen.
Don't use Primary button for destructive actions — use Secondary with red text.
Don't use CTA-style labels longer than 3 words on small buttons.
Don't reduce opacity for disabled state — use explicit disabled token colors.
Don't use icon-only buttons without a tooltip or context label.
Button.usageContext: Use Button for actions. Use Pill for filters/selections. Use Infobanner CTA for contextual inline actions.

t3t4Constraints.labelLength: Keep under 4 words for readability on small screens
t3t4Constraints.contrast: Primary button must maintain 4.5:1 contrast ratio on white background

Badge.description: Non-interactive visual indicator for status, counts, or categorical information.
doRules: Use positive (green) for confirmed/completed states.
Use negative (red) for error, rejected, or out-of-stock states.
Use highlight (blue) for informational, new, or featured states.
Keep badge labels short — ideally 1-2 words or a number.
dontRules: Don't make badges clickable — use Pill component for interactive filters.
Don't use more than 2 badges on a single card.
Don't use custom color variants outside the defined 6-color system.
Badge.usageContext: Use Badge for non-interactive status display. Use Pill for clickable filters. Use Infobanner for page-level messages.

InputField.description: Allows users to enter text. Uses an underlined (not outlined) style for clear affordance with minimal visual weight.
doRules: Always show a visible label above the input field.
Use supporting text for format hints (e.g. '10-digit mobile number').
Error state must show red underline AND error message text below.
Active state indicator (underline) must be brand color (#9F2089).
dontRules: Don't use placeholder text as the only label — it disappears on focus.
Don't use outlined/boxed input style — MESH uses underline style only.
Don't place more than one CTA action inside a single input field.
InputField.usageContext: Use for all text data collection. For numeric quantity changes use InputStepper. For option selection use Dropdown variant.

Infobanner.description: Contextual message that distinguishes important information from surrounding body text.
doRules: Match semantic color to message type: green=success, red=error, yellow=warning, blue=info.
Use Big size when the message has a multi-line label or requires emphasis.
Use Inset variant inside cards or contained surfaces.
dontRules: Don't use more than one Infobanner on the same screen.
Don't use Infobanner for page-level errors — use Snackbar instead.
Don't use the error Infobanner for warnings — semantic color must match message intent.
Infobanner.usageContext: Use for inline contextual messages. Use Snackbar for transient system feedback. Use Pop-Up for blocking decisions.

PopUp.description: Modal dialog that interrupts the user flow to present critical information or require a decision.
doRules: Use sparingly — only when user must make a decision to continue.
Always provide at least one dismissal action.
Tapping the overlay or Android back button must dismiss the pop-up.
dontRules: Don't use for non-critical information — use Infobanner or Snackbar instead.
Don't stack more than one pop-up.
Don't use more than 2 action buttons in a single pop-up.
PopUp.usageContext: Use when the action is irreversible (delete, cancel order) or requires acknowledgment. For non-blocking info use Snackbar.

Loader.description: Indicates loading or processing state. Uses the Meesho brand logo mark as the spinner.
doRules: Show body text when the expected wait time is longer than 3 seconds.
Always use the dark overlay when displaying over app content.
dontRules: Don't use the Loader for skeleton states — build skeleton screens separately.
Don't use the 'm' logo as the AI Indicator — it is reserved for the Loader component.
Loader.usageContext: Use for full-screen blocking loading states. Use skeleton screens for content placeholders.

Snackbar.description: Transient, non-blocking notification shown at the bottom of the screen. Auto-dismisses.
doRules: Auto-dismiss after 3-4 seconds.
Keep message under 60 characters.
Use positive (green) background for success confirmations.
dontRules: Don't use for critical errors that require user action — use Pop-Up.
Don't show more than one Snackbar at a time.
Snackbar.usageContext: Use for transient system feedback (item added to cart, address saved). Use Pop-Up for decisions. Use Infobanner for persistent contextual messages.

BottomNav.description: Persistent bottom navigation bar with 4-5 top-level destinations. Each destination has an icon and label.
doRules: Use filled icons for selected state, outlined icons for unselected.
Always show text labels alongside icons.
Bottom Nav hides on scroll down and reappears on scroll up.
dontRules: Don't use more than 5 or fewer than 4 destinations.
Don't use Bottom Nav on secondary screens — only on top-level destinations.
BottomNav.usageContext: Use for the app's 4-5 primary sections. Use Tabs for secondary section switching within a page.

TopNav.description: App bar at the top of each screen providing title, navigation, and contextual actions.
doRules: Top Nav hides on scroll down and reappears when user scrolls up.
Use Back+Primary CTA variant only when the screen has a single primary action.
User profile avatar appears only on the Home variant.
dontRules: Don't show more than 3 action icons in the right zone.
Don't use Top Nav for bottom sheets or modal overlays.
TopNav.usageContext: Required on all full-page screens. Not used inside bottom sheets or modals.
